boj_gradual_hiking_cycle.claim: The Bank of Japan is in a gradual tightening cycle, raising rates in small increments after ending negative rates in March 2024.

provenance.definition: The Bank of Japan is in a gradual tightening cycle, raising rates in small increments after ending negative rates in March 2024.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: BOJ raised policy rate from -0.1% to 0-0.1% in March 2024, then to 0.25% in July 2024, then to 0.5% in January 2025, then to 0.75% in March 2025.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.75%.

dissenting_pressure_for_hike.claim: Multiple board members have consistently dissented in favor of a rate hike, indicating internal pressure for tightening.

provenance.definition: Multiple board members have consistently dissented in favor of a rate hike, indicating internal pressure for tightening.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, three members proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but were defeated 6-3.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

market_expectations_elevated.claim: Market participants and economists widely expect a rate hike at the June meeting, with Polymarket probability at 63%.

provenance.definition: Market participants and economists widely expect a rate hike at the June meeting, with Polymarket probability at 63%.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Reuters reported that the BOJ has 'locked in' signals for a June rate hike, and nearly two-thirds of economists expect 1.0% by end-June.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan has locked in signals for a June rate hike, with nearly two-thirds of economists expecting the rate to reach 1.0% by end-June.

nakagawa_junko.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold

provenance.definition: Nakagawa Junko votes against maintaining the current rate and proposes a higher rate.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Nakagawa Junko dissents from a decision to keep the rate unchanged.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Votes where she supports a hold or is absent.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Nakagawa Junko proposed raising the rate to 1.0% and dissented from the hold decision.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

ueda_kazuo.quantity: this governor votes with the majority on rate decisions

provenance.definition: Governor Ueda votes in favor of the final decision, whether to hold or raise.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Ueda supports the majority decision.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Governor Ueda voted with the majority to keep the rate unchanged at 0.75%.
cases.basis_quote: The Policy Board voted 6-3 to keep the rate unchanged.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: BOJ raises short-term policy interest rate at June 15-16, 2026 meeting

provenance.definition: The BOJ announces a guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate higher than the current 0.75%.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any increase in the rate, e.g., to 1.0% or higher.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 3 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the March 18-19, 2025 meeting, the BOJ raised the rate from 0.5% to 0.75%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.75%.

cases.description: At the January 23-24, 2025 meeting, the BOJ raised the rate from 0.25% to 0.5%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.5%.

cases.description: At the July 30-31, 2024 meeting, the BOJ raised the rate from 0-0.1% to 0.25%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.25%.

boj_policy_board.consequence_of_nonparticipation: Non-participating members do not affect the vote count; quorum must be met.
